data.title: As a team using DSO with Jira, relates_to links round-trip correctly between local and Jira
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Verify end-to-end round-trip of relates_to links: local-to-Jira via outbound bridge and Jira-to-local via inbound bridge.
**Why**: The epic requires verified round-trip behavior to ensure link sync works in both directions without drift or duplication.
**Scope**:
- IN: Integration test that creates a local relates_to link, runs outbound sync, confirms link appears in Jira; integration test that creates a Jira Relates link, runs inbound sync, confirms it appears locally; timestamp ordering within sync batches for LINK/UNLINK events
- OUT: Unit-level testing of individual functions (covered by other stories)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, an integration test demonstrates that a local relates_to link created via CLI and synced outbound appears as a Relates link in Jira
  <- Satisfies: "Round-trip verified: create a local relates_to link via CLI, run ticket sync, confirm the link appears in Jira"
- When this story is complete, an integration test demonstrates that a Jira Relates link synced inbound appears as a local relates_to relationship
  <- Satisfies: "Round-trip verified: create a Relates link in Jira, run ticket sync, confirm it appears locally"
- When this story is complete, LINK and UNLINK events within a sync batch are processed in timestamp order
  <- Satisfies: "Process events in timestamp order within each sync batch"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Round-trip tests should use mock Jira API, not live Jira instance
- [Testing] Coordinate with existing test patterns in test_bridge_outbound_integration.py and test_bridge_inbound_integration.py
